mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) erode the real value of fixed-income securities, compelling investors to demand higher nominal yields as compensation for rising inflation expectations, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL monthly change against 20-day forward yield change is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period, or the sign of the correlation is negative in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate signals economic weakness and triggers a flight-to-safety into long-term government bonds, lowering yields as investors seek safe assets, especially when the unemployment rate crosses above a key threshold of 4.5%.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% from a level below 4.0% within 3 months
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE level changes against 20-day forward yield changes is less than 0.03, or the average yield change in triggered instances is not negative at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate robust aggregate demand and supply-side expansion, reinforcing expectations of higher future growth and inflation, which pushes long-term yields upward over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO are in the top quartile of their respective 12-month growth rates, the subsequent 60-day yield return is not positive in at least 60% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, the Federal Reserve raises the fed funds rate to curb inflation; this policy action eventually slows economic growth and lowers inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over a multi-month horizon as the market prices in a slower future economy.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 6 months, the average 60-day forward yield change is not negative at a 95% confidence level, or the signal fails in a similar tightening episode from history.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply capacity, which can reduce upward price pressures and inflation expectations; as a result, the 10-year Treasury yield tends to decline as the real equilibrium interest rate decreases and term premiums shrink.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change against 20-day forward yield change is below 0.03 or positive in sign over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the fed funds rate (e.g., 25 bp or more in one month) signals an unexpected monetary easing, which immediately lowers short-term rate expectations and pulls down the entire yield curve, including the 10-year Treasury yield, over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 25 bp month-over-month
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS drops ≥25 bp, the average 20-day forward yield change is not negative at a 95% confidence level, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based labor market and housing sector expansion, which reinforces expectations of sustained economic growth and rising inflation, leading to higher 10-year Treasury yields over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and HOUST are in the top third of their 12-month growth rates simultaneously, the subsequent 20-day yield return is not positive in at least 55% of such cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in monthly CPI (more than 1% month-over-month) shocks inflation expectations and forces the bond market to reprice term premiums upward, causing an immediate rise in the 10-year Treasury yield over the following week as investors demand higher compensation.
failure_condition: In months with CPI month-over-month increase ≥1%, the 5-day forward yield change is not positive in at least 60% of observations, or the average change is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p < 0.10).
